text:
(Only include the appropriate paragraphs in respect of the investigation, charge, and what applications have been made in respect of disclosure)

[Name of police force] are investigating an allegation of [offence(s)] by [name(s)].

[Name] has been charged on [date] with [offence(s)]. That matter is listed for [hearing type in criminal proceedings] on [date].

[Name] applied for an order restraining any party from disclosing the judgments dated [date(s)] to the police.

[Name of police force] have requested disclosure from these proceedings by making an Annex 6 request of the local authority on [date] and applying within these proceedings on [date], and such application was served on the parties.

(If a party consents to the disclosure that fact should be recited)

[Name(s)] [has] / [have] consented to the documents listed in this order being disclosed to [name of police force].

This order is made without prejudice to section 12 of the Administration of Justice Act 1960 (as amended by chapter 7 of Part 12 of the Family Procedure Rules 2010 and Practice Direction 12G: and section 98(2) of Children Act 1989 as amended).

It is recorded that any unauthorised disclosure of the judgments named in this order would be a contempt of court.

IT IS ORDERED [BY CONSENT] THAT:

The application by [name] for an order restraining any party from disclosing the judgment[s] dated [date(s)] is refused.

The local authority may disclose to [name of police force] for use in relation to the current criminal investigation the following:

The judgments in these proceedings of [date(s)].

All documents in the court bundle for case number [case number] as listed in the index [dated] / [to the bundle for the hearing on [date]].

[Or insert a list of specific documents]

[Name of police force] are permitted to disclose the information and documents listed at paragraph [para number] to the Crown Prosecution Service to inform any charging decision and for use in any ensuing criminal prosecution.

Should there be a prosecution of a party in these proceedings, [name of police force] and the Crown Prosecution Service are permitted to disclose the information and documents listed at paragraph [para number] to the defence solicitors of that party.
